figures.caption: FIG. 1. Generic VQC architecture with data re-uploading. A classical input sample x is encoded into a quantum state through a data-dependent unitary Uφ(x), where the rotation angles φi(x) are functions of the input features. The symbol φ denotes the data-encoding functions, not trainable model parameters. Each encoding block is followed by a trainable variational block Uθ, composed of parameterized gates such as Rn(θ) and entangling gates. Here, Rn denotes a generic singlequbit rotation around n-axis in the Bloch sphere, and each occurrence of θ represents a trainable scalar parameter contained in the full parameter vector θ. The encoding–variational structure is repeated for L layers, allowing the circuit to reintroduce the classical data multiple times. After the final layer, Pauli-Z expectation values are measured to form the classical feature vector z = (⟨Z1⟩, . . . , ⟨Znq⟩). A classical decision function and loss L(z, y) compare the model output with the true label y, and a classical optimizer updates the trainable parameters θ.
TABLE I. Preprocessing and training hyperparameters for all one-vs-one tasks.
FIG. 3. Mean per-class test accuracy averaged over five seeds.
TABLE III. Per-class mean ± 95% CI (%) over five seeds for SVM baselines: linear SVM, SVM-QK (trained), and SVM-RBF.
TABLE IV. Per-class mean ± 95% CI (%) over five seeds comparing the VQC to the SVM-QK (trained) derived from the same trained circuit (frozen parameters).

FIG. 6. Average VQC test accuracy versus qubit count on PCA-32 inputs, averaged over all 45 EuroSAT-MS class pairs. Error bars show 95% Student-t confidence intervals across the pairwise mean accuracies. Horizontal lines indicate the average linear-SVM and SVM-RBF accuracies on the same benchmark.
FIG. 5. Mean per-class test accuracy averaged over five seeds comparing the VQC and an SVM-QK (trained) induced by the same trained PQC (parameters frozen).

FIG. 2. Four-qubit PQC used in this work. The initialization block contains parameter-only rotations, while each data re-uploading block applies input-dependent rotations followed by fixed CNOT entangling layers. The gate labels Ry(α), Rz(β), and Rx(γ) are schematic shorthand: in block ℓ and qubit q, they correspond respectively to Ry(αℓq(x;θ)), Rz(βℓq(x;θ)), and Rx(γℓq(x;θ)), where the angles are affine functions of selected input features with trainable coefficients. The data re-uploading block is repeated six times, with entangling patterns alternating between A and B.
TABLE II. Per-class mean ± 95% CI (%) over five seeds for discriminative models (LogReg, NN, and VQC).
FIG. 4. Mean per-class test accuracy averaged over five seeds comparing SVM-linear, SVM-QK (trained), and SVM-RBF.
